fact: June 11 is exactly 162 days into the year, which means there are exactly 203 days left - that's almost perfectly splitting the year in half!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when fireflies start their crazy light shows at dusk, blinking like tiny disco balls in your backyard!
The sun rises before 5:30 AM in most northern places on June 11, so early that even roosters are still sleepy!
June 11 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when the zodiac twins are supposedly making everyone extra chatty and curious about everything.
In Australia and other southern places, June 11 is deep winter, and wombats are getting extra chunky with their thick winter fur coats!
This date sits just 10 days before the summer solstice, so the sun is climbing to its highest point and making shadows super short at noon.
June 11 is prime time for strawberries to turn bright red and juicy in many places - they're literally bursting with sweetness right now!
In northern countries like Alaska, the sun barely sets on June 11, creating an eerie twilight that lasts almost all night long!
Baby birds are getting kicked out of their nests around this time - June 11 is peak 'learn to fly or else' season for most songbirds!
The moon can look extra orange or red when it rises on June 11 because it's traveling through thicker summer air filled with dust and pollen.
In many places, June 11 marks when cicadas start their deafening buzz concerts that can be louder than a lawn mower!
This is when dandelions have mostly turned into those fluffy white puffballs that explode into a million flying seeds when you blow on them!
